body_txt: Like garlic , anchovies , fruitcake , and pineapple on pizza , raisins have a pretty bad reputation in media as a stock "gross" food. They're wrinkly, shriveled, have a jarringly sticky texture, and worst of all is when they're used in place of chocolate chips in cookies or given out on Halloween by health-conscious neighbors. Ironically, raisins were once considered as a treat to provide a burst of sweetness in meals and desserts; now that sugar and sweeteners are widely available and inexpensive, raisins are a bit of an artifact. This trope often involves someone unexpectedly eating food that has raisins in it (usually an oatmeal raisin cookie assuming that it's chocolate chip), much to their disgust. Expect characters who don't like raisins to compare them to rabbit poop or something similar, or complain about how annoying it is to pick them out of a different food. Characters who do like them will try to bring up that they're healthy note Oatmeal raisin cookies have the same sugar content as chocolate chip, if not slightly more due to the added natural sugars of the fruit. Reducing or removing the artificial sugars can make the oatmeal raisin cookies healthier. , or offer them as an alternative to sugary sweets, only to get shot down. This trope is averted in winemaking, as wine made from raisins provide more flavor and sweetness than fresh grapes, and due to the labor-intensive process of drying the grapes, raisin wines are more expensive, therefore more classy . A subtrope of Stock "Yuck!" . Other related tropes include:
